summary: Model went 4-6 on direction (40% hit rate) across 10 graded matchups; MIA@MIN was correctly withheld as PENDING_SP_DATA. The two staked picks both won: PHI@BOS GOLD CONFIRM (3-1 W) and DET@NYM OVERRIDE to NYM (9-4 W). HARD CAP 1 correctly prevented staking on KC@CHW and CHC@ATL, both of which went the wrong direction. HARD CAP 6 correctly blocked HOU PLATINUM (SEA won 8-3). Key learning: negative-edge GOLD picks continue to lose reliably (5th and 6th validated applications); three-signal GOLD bar is now 4-for-4 in the archive.

COL @ PIT.headline: PIT dominated COL 7-2; F3=3579.3 (slate's largest) correctly identified the directional edge, but no stake was placed due to missing fair_prob.
COL @ PIT.hypothesis: PIT's 7-2 blowout validates F3_swing_take_gap=3579.3 as a genuine signal. The missing fair_prob correctly capped grade at C and zeroed the stake — this was a direction WIN but the no-stake decision was process-correct given the market validation requirement. PIT's home advantage and extreme swing-take gap were real, but without a market anchor the exposure was appropriately zero. This is favorable variance with sound process: the signal was real, the guard rail was correctly applied.
COL @ PIT.signals_to_recheck: fair_prob missing correctly excluded staking on a valid directional win; no recalibration needed — the market validation requirement is functioning as designed

WSH @ CIN.headline: CIN demolished WSH 15-1; D-grade SKIP correctly identified low conviction, no stake was placed.
WSH @ CIN.hypothesis: The 15-1 blowout confirms CIN had a dominant structural advantage that the model partially captured (SP edge against WSH, PQI against WSH at Δ=+17.0). WSH at 50.89% was essentially a coin flip, and the coin landed badly. The D grade (-2) and SKIP tier correctly excluded any stake exposure. No process failure: low-conviction SKIP picks losing by blowout margins is expected variance, not a systematic model error. The model's directional read on WSH was wrong, consistent with D-grade expected accuracy.
WSH @ CIN.signals_to_recheck: SP edge 0.92 xERA AGAINST the pick and PQI against at +17.0 both predicted this outcome; D-grade SKIP with no F-signals and opposing SP advantage is the correct posture — enforce without stake

DET @ NYM.headline: NYM won 9-4; Claude's OVERRIDE to NYM was fully vindicated — the DET@NYM 05-12 analogy (f5_full_delta=0.2931 ≈ 0.3285) produced the same NYM dominant outcome.
DET @ NYM.hypothesis: NYM's 9-4 win mirrors the 05-12 DET@NYM result (10-2 NYM) almost exactly. All four OVERRIDE conditions converged: extreme f5_full_delta=0.2931 (NYM wins F5 at 75.4%), stacked pipeline flags (acute_roster=True + confidence_downgrade=True), SP edge 0.99 xERA against DET, and market pricing DET at only 46.09%. The direct analogy to the prior DET@NYM context was correct, and Claude's OVERRIDE to NYM produced the second consecutive NYM-direction staked winner in this matchup. This is the sixth validated OVERRIDE application in the postgame archive. Process win; no modification needed.
DET @ NYM.signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta=0.2931 with stacked flags remains the highest-confidence OVERRIDE trigger; the DET@NYM matchup context has now produced two consecutive OVERRIDE wins, suggesting a persistent structural model misread on DET's full-game prospects when NYM holds the F5 advantage

MIA @ MIN.headline: MIN dominated MIA 9-1; PENDING_SP_DATA designation was correct — zero Statcast data on both starters prevented any directional pick.
MIA @ MIN.hypothesis: MIN's 9-1 blowout reveals that MIN held a significant advantage the model could not quantify without SP quality data. Both Zebby Matthews and Braxton Garrett had 0 Statcast pitches season-to-date. The PENDING designation correctly withheld any directional call. Had the model forced a pick, the 9-1 margin suggests MIN was the right side, but the outcome was not reliably predictable from available data. This is the fifth PENDING_SP_DATA validation in the postgame archive — the protocol remains appropriate.
MIA @ MIN.signals_to_recheck: small_sample_SP_True; 0-Statcast-pitch starters continue to warrant PENDING regardless of other signals — the lopsided result reinforces that SP quality is load-bearing for directional accuracy

SD @ MIL.headline: MIL crushed SD 7-1; no F-signals and PQI against the pick correctly identified a weak directional call.
SD @ MIL.hypothesis: MIL's 7-1 win reflects genuine structural advantages that the model's signals partially identified: PQI against SD (Δ=+3.4) and no active F-signals were correct warnings. SD's 50.76% pick probability was barely above a coin flip and the outcome was a blowout in the wrong direction. SKIP tier correctly excluded any stake. No process failure: a 51% SKIP pick losing by six runs is expected variance and the SKIP designation provided full protection from exposure.
SD @ MIL.signals_to_recheck: PQI against the pick correctly predicted MIL's advantage; no F-signals on a ~51% pick with fair_prob missing — SKIP is the only correct tier in this configuration

SEA @ HOU.headline: SEA won 8-3 despite HOU holding the SP advantage; HARD CAP 6 correctly blocked the PLATINUM pick and prevented what would have been a large staked loss.
SEA @ HOU.hypothesis: SEA's 8-3 win is the third consecutive SEA victory over HOU in recent series games, and this time without an elite opposing SP as the primary driver (F1_xera_gap=2.51 favored HOU today). HARD CAP 6 correctly fired at edge=+48.11pp (calibrator hallucination range), and fair_prob=0.1693 — the market pricing HOU at only ~17% — was an extreme warning signal that the model was fundamentally misreading this matchup. Claude correctly identified that OVERRIDE conditions were not met (the F1 gap favored HOU, not SEA), but the market's 17% implied probability for HOU suggests acute roster disruption or team-quality factors beyond the model's current inputs. Process-sound SKIP; the loss direction reinforces that HOU's model inputs may be structurally unreliable in this series context.
SEA @ HOU.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 6] extreme_positive_edge_hallucination; additionally, fair_prob=0.1693 (well below 0.42 floor) on a PLATINUM pick signals near-certain model artifact — when fair_prob < 0.25 on any PLATINUM or GOLD pick, treat as automatic SKIP regardless of F-signal count or SP advantage

STL @ OAK.headline: STL edged OAK 5-4 in a one-run game; model direction correct on a true coin-flip pick.
STL @ OAK.hypothesis: STL's 5-4 win is pure variance on a D-grade 50.93% pick — the model and market both saw this as near a coin flip (market had STL at 47.14%), and STL won the close game. SP edge 1.71 xERA against STL and PQI against STL (Δ=+23.2) both suggested OAK had structural advantages, yet the game was a one-run STL road win. SKIP tier was correct: no stake was placed, and a directional loss on a 51% D-grade pick would have been equally expected. No process lesson to extract from a coin flip going right.
STL @ OAK.signals_to_recheck: SP edge and PQI both pointed to OAK; STL's 5-4 win is variance on a genuine coin-flip, not model accuracy — D-grade SKIP picks winning does not generate pattern updates

PHI @ BOS.headline: PHI won 3-1 — the cleanest staked winner on the slate, validating the three-signal GOLD bar for the fourth consecutive time.
PHI @ BOS.hypothesis: PHI's 3-1 victory is a clean process win. F1_xera_gap=0.93* (small-sample asterisk) backed by PQI confirming PHI (Δ=-7.9) satisfied HARD CAP 5's secondary-signal requirement. Edge=+5.6pp within [4,15]pp band, f5_full_delta=0.1189 well below the 0.15 concern threshold, no stacked pipeline flags. The three-signal GOLD bar (F-signal fire + positive edge + gap < 0.15 + no flags) has now produced wins in all four applications to date. The PHI margin (3-1) is consistent with a genuine 55.5% pick probability — not a lucky blowout but a validly-calibrated small edge realized. Process win without caveats.
PHI @ BOS.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap* + PQI backup satisfying HARD CAP 5 is validated for the second time; this combination is sufficient for GOLD CONFIRM when edge is positive within band and gap is below 0.15

CHC @ ATL.headline: CHC shut out ATL 2-0; negative edge (-12.86pp) correctly signaled market held better information about CHC's pitching superiority.
CHC @ ATL.hypothesis: ATL was shut out completely, validating the edge=-12.86pp as a reliable predictor of market knowledge the model lacked. The F3=1518.9 signal correctly identified ATL's lineup swing-take advantage but could not overcome CHC's starting pitcher, who was elite enough to neutralize that lineup edge. This is a process observation worth tracking: when edge is deeply negative (< -8pp) AND the opposing SP holds a quality advantage (xERA 0.49 against the pick means very strong performance), the F3 signal is describing lineup potential that the opposing pitcher is capable of suppressing. HARD CAP 1 context correctly zeroed ATL's stake — no real exposure. The direction loss is the pattern signal here.
CHC @ ATL.signals_to_recheck: negative_edge_GOLD with SP edge 0.49 xERA AGAINST the pick; when edge < -8pp and opposing SP xERA is below 3.5, treat the F-signal fire as insufficient to overcome market consensus — the market correctly priced CHC's pitching superiority that the model underweighted

KC @ CHW.headline: CWS won 6-2; HARD CAP 1 correctly prevented staking on a negative-edge PLATINUM pick — KC's strong F-signals could not overcome the market's negative-edge read.
KC @ CHW.hypothesis: CWS won convincingly 6-2 despite KC holding strong F-signals (F1=2.97, F3=2313.3) and a large positive PQI (Δ=-28.9 confirming KC). The negative edge (-5.67pp) was the decisive warning: the market priced KC at 56.55% while the model only gave KC 50.88%, meaning the market was more bullish on KC than the model AND KC still lost. HARD CAP 1 (negative_edge_GOLD) fired correctly and demoted the pre-cap A- to B-, zeroing the stake. This is the 5th and 6th validated applications of HARD CAP 1 in today's archive alone (KC@CHW and CHC@ATL). The F-signals were real but the negative edge correctly flagged superior market information about CWS's ability to counter KC's SP advantage.
KC @ CHW.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative_edge_GOLD; F1=2.97 and F3=2313.3 are strong signals that still lost against a negative edge — confirm that HARD CAP 1 continues to be enforced even when F-signal strength appears overwhelming

SF @ LAD.headline: LAD won 5-2; model direction correct despite D-grade and model_prob outside valid range — SKIP was appropriately applied.
SF @ LAD.hypothesis: LAD's 5-2 win with the model correctly identifying LAD's direction is a variance WIN — the D grade (-4) and SKIP designation were correct process calls given model_prob=0.7588 outside [0.48, 0.72] and f5_full_delta=0.3097 (SF wins F5). LAD needed to overcome SF's early starter advantage and succeed in the back half of the game, which is exactly what happened. However, the pipeline correctly SKIPped this game due to the extreme model_prob, and the SKIP designation provided appropriate stake-free posture. A direction win at D grade is not a process vindication — the SKIP was right.
SF @ LAD.signals_to_recheck: model_prob outside [0.48, 0.72] correctly triggered SKIP; f5_full_delta=0.3097 was a structural risk (SF dominates F5) that did not materialize — LAD direction win here is favorable variance, not F5-gap failure

patterns_observed: Three-signal GOLD CONFIRM bar (F-signal fire + positive edge_pp > 4pp + f5_full_delta < 0.15 + no stacked flags) is now 4-for-4 in the postgame archive (TB@TOR 05-11, ARI@TEX 05-11, WSH@CIN 05-12, PHI@BOS 05-14). When F1_xera_gap carries a small-sample asterisk, PQI backup satisfying HARD CAP 5 is sufficient to sustain the GOLD CONFIRM. The bar is correctly calibrated — confirm GOLD whenever all four conditions clear without exception.

HARD CAP 1 (negative_edge_GOLD) is now validated 5 times in the archive: today alone fired correctly on both KC@CHW (CWS won 6-2, edge=-5.67pp) and CHC@ATL (CHC won 2-0, edge=-12.86pp). Every negative-edge GOLD or PLATINUM pick in the archive has produced a staking loss when the pick went active. Enforce HARD CAP 1 without exception regardless of F-signal strength — even F1=2.97 and F3=2313.3 stacking together (KC@CHW) failed to override the market's negative-edge signal.

DET@NYM OVERRIDE methodology (sixth validation): f5_full_delta=0.2931 in the same matchup context as 05-12 (0.3285) produced a nearly identical NYM dominant win (9-4 vs 10-2). The direct f5_full_delta analogy within the same matchup is a reliable OVERRIDE trigger when combined with stacked pipeline flags. This is the sixth consecutive OVERRIDE validation in the archive — maintain the four-condition trigger without modification.

CHC@ATL establishes that opposing SP quality can neutralize large F3 signals when the market already holds that information: F3=1518.9 fired for ATL's lineup advantage, yet CHC's pitcher shut ATL out 2-0. The key predictor was not the F3 signal but the edge=-12.86pp: the market's large discount on ATL correctly reflected CHC's pitching edge. Going forward, when edge < -8pp on a GOLD pick AND SP edge is against the pick direction, treat the F-signal as describing unrealizable lineup potential — market information dominates.
